Ad Text:
National Affordable Housing (NAH) is a not‑for‑profit social value enterprise working to create better lives through the delivery of safe, affordable and secure housing. We partner with governments, developers and community organisations to deliver housing outcomes that make a real difference for people and communities.

 




About the Role

We are seeking a Property Analyst to join our Program Development & Delivery team and support the assessment, acquisition and delivery of new Social and Affordable Housing opportunities.

This role plays a key part across the program development lifecycle, from early opportunity assessment and financial analysis through to capital and debt funding, construction monitoring, settlement and handover. Working closely with internal teams and external partners, you will help ensure new housing opportunities are financially sustainable, aligned with NAH’s values, and deliverable within agreed funding and risk settings.

Key Responsibilities: 
•    Assess new housing opportunities, including established properties, off the plan and turnkey development acquisitions
•    Undertake property, partner and program assessments to ensure suitability for target cohorts
•    Develop and maintain financial and feasibility models, including operating cost and debt analysis
•    Support acquisition due diligence, approvals and governance processes
•    Prepare capital funding and debt submissions for both government and external funders
•    Monitor turnkey developments during construction, tracking milestones, risks and delivery progress
•    Support completion, settlement and structured handover to asset management and operations teams
•    Maintain high quality data, analysis and reporting across the program pipeline
 




About You

You are a strong analytical thinker with a genuine interest in Social and Affordable Housing. You bring a mix of financial capability, commercial awareness and collaborative working style, and are comfortable engaging with a range of stakeholders.

You will ideally have:

Strong financial modelling and analytical capability (advanced Excel essential)
Experience in property, housing, development, acquisitions, or program delivery environments
Understanding of housing funding models, affordability, debt and financial sustainability
High attention to detail and sound risk awareness
The ability to support senior decision‑makers with clear, evidence‑based advice

Tertiary qualifications in property, finance, economics, planning, construction or a related discipline are desirable, along with experience in community or affordable housing environments.

At Intelligen, we’re building something different. Over the past three years, we’ve evolved from a bold startup to a trusted partner for some of Australia’s most complex data transformations. We’ve helped organisations move from legacy to modern platforms, embedded governance into decision-making, and brought AI into the hands of the business - responsibly and at speed.

As we continue to grow, we’re looking for senior leaders who can operate at the intersection of advanced analytics, data strategy, and modern architecture. This is a role for someone who doesn’t just design technical solutions, but helps clients navigate ambiguity, shape strategy, and build secure, scalable data capability that delivers measurable business value.

We’re seeking a Principal Data Consultant to lead high-impact engagements where analytics, insight, and enterprise data architecture converge, bringing trusted advisory capability, deep technical expertise, and delivery leadership to some of the most important transformation programs in market.

About the Role

We are seeking a Principal Data Consultant who can design, deliver, and scale advanced analytics and insight solutions in complex enterprise environments.

This role combines deep technical expertise with strategic advisory capability. You will lead high-impact engagements where data, analytics, and architecture intersect, helping organisations translate ambitious goals into secure, production-grade platforms and decisioning capabilities that drive real outcomes.

You will operate as both architect and trusted advisor, guiding executive stakeholders through the evolution of their analytics maturity while ensuring solutions align to secure-by-design principles, strong governance, and modern data management practices.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in complexity, can lead multidisciplinary teams across data engineering, analytics, and platform architecture, and is motivated by solving important client problems with clarity, rigour, and commercial focus.

What You’ll Do

Partner with senior executives to identify high-value opportunities for advanced analytics, reporting, and decision intelligence
Translate strategic business objectives into scalable, secure data and analytics architectures
Guide organisations through analytics transformation, managing governance, regulatory, risk, and compliance considerations
Architect end-to-end analytics platforms, including data pipelines, semantic models, real-time analytics, forecasting, decision support, and insight delivery
Lead the implementation of secure data and analytics systems across modern cloud environments such as AWS, Azure, and GCP
Embed security, privacy, and governance into architecture and delivery decisions from the outset
Design resilient analytics ecosystems that support trusted decision-making, operational performance, and long-term scalability
Lead complex transformation engagements from strategy through execution
Mentor consultants, engineers, and client teams across multidisciplinary delivery environments
Translate technical complexity into clear decision frameworks for executives and senior stakeholders
Support the growth of Intelligen’s advanced analytics and insights capability through thought leadership, client engagement, and delivery excellence



Requirements



10+ years’ experience delivering advanced analytics, insights, or data transformation solutions in enterprise environments
Proven experience designing and implementing production-grade analytics solutions, with exposure to AI-enabled use cases where relevant
Strong expertise across modern data platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, or similar ecosystems
Deep understanding of cloud-native architecture across AWS, Azure, and/or GCP
Demonstrated leadership in large consulting, advisory, or transformation engagements
Strong ability to work with both executive stakeholders and highly technical teams
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to simplify complexity and guide decision-making at senior levels
A mindset grounded in strategic thinking, delivery leadership, and measurable value creation

Core Principles for This Role

At Intelligen, we evaluate leaders not only on what they deliver, but how they think, operate, and create impact. For this role, we are especially looking for evidence of the following:

Judgment Under Ambiguity

The ability to make strong decisions with incomplete information, navigate uncertainty, and create direction in complex environments.

Insight-Led Thinking

A consistent ability to connect data, context, and business priorities to generate clear, actionable insight that supports better decision-making.

Secure-by-Design Thinking

A disciplined approach to embedding security, governance, privacy, and risk into architecture and delivery decisions from day one.

Value Creation

A focus on measurable business outcomes, ensuring strategy, analytics, and technology choices translate into meaningful impact.

Trajectory

Evidence that the candidate can already operate one level above the role, with the leadership maturity and strategic capability to scale with the business and our clients.

Systems Thinking

The ability to connect strategy, data, analytics, operations, and architecture into coherent solutions that work in practice — not just in theory.



Benefits



We’re not just delivering AI and data projects.. we’re humanising them. That means we care deeply about the how, not just the what. We value curiosity, creativity, and a willingness to challenge the status quo. We look for people who are driven to build a business, get curious, and offer opinions and ideas.

You’ll join a team that’s smart, kind, and ambitious. You’ll have real influence - in your projects, your practice, and the broader business. And as we grow, so will you.

We’re not just delivering AI and data projects.. we’re humanising them. That means we care deeply about the how, not just the what. We value curiosity, creativity, and a willingness to challenge the status quo. We look for people who are driven to build a business, get curious, and offer opinions and ideas.

You’ll join a team that’s smart, kind, and ambitious. You’ll have real influence - in your projects, your practice, and the broader business. And as we grow, so will you. Here’s what that looks like:

Grow With Purpose

Support That Helps You Thrive

A Community You’ll Enjoy Being Part Of

About the Role

We're seeking a Senior Commercial Analyst to play a critical role in strengthening OPD's commercial governance and maximising value under the Franchise Agreement. Reporting to the Commercial Manager, this role leads the uplift and end‑to‑end management of Schedule 3 processes and claims, ensuring submissions are accurate, defensible, timely and optimised for MTM outcomes.

This is a highly influential role that works closely with MTM, DTP, Strategic Partnering, Finance and operational leaders to protect value and support strong commercial decision‑making.

This is a max term opportunity for 2 year period.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the uplift, redesign and end‑to‑end management of Schedule 3 processes, claims and governance frameworks
Build and manage a structured Schedule 3 claims pipeline, ensuring visibility, prioritisation and contractual compliance
Prepare and submit high‑quality, defensible Schedule 3 claims that are accurate, well supported and audit‑ready
Prepare or support preparation of other commercial claims including DEAP, SAP, Major Events and labour‑related submissions
Act as a key conduit between MTM and DTP, providing clear and transparent explanations of claim methodologies, calculations and assumptions
Partner with Strategic Partnering, Senior FBPs and operational leaders to deliver commercial insight supporting budgeting, forecasting and value protection
Strengthen governance, controls, documentation and process consistency, identifying opportunities to reduce risk, leakage and manual effort

Qualifications & Experience

Demonstrated experience in commercial, financial or contract management roles within complex operational environments
Demonstrated experience in commercial, operational or financial analysis
Advanced modelling and data interpretation skills
Strong understanding of financial controls, governance frameworks and key cost drivers
Experience documenting processes and improving consistency across commercial workflows
Ability to work effectively with non‑financial stakeholders and influence decision‑making
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Exposure to roster costing methodologies or workforce analytics (desirable)

Next Steps

About the role:

Ready to be part of a high-impact, business-wide transformation in one of Australia's fastest-evolving energy markets? We are looking for a data specialist who understands how information flows across an organisation to set up future capabilities. You will have not only technical ability but extremely strong interpersonal skills and business process understanding. Your expertise goes beyond the surface; you possess a strong grasp of data models and have a proven track record of delivering on projects specifically focused on data and reporting. Our preferred candidate will be able to demonstrate the following skills and experience:

5+ years of experience as a Business Analyst, with a preference for experience in the retail energy sector.
Proven ability to deliver major business solutions, including application testing and implementation.
Highly developed stakeholder management skills and proven resilience.
Demonstrated commitment to continuous improvement in process design.
Experience in delivering business solutions using both Agile and Waterfall methodologies.
Strong proficiency in SQL for independent data collection and analysis.
Familiarity with collaborative tools, including Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Slides) and Atlassian products (Confluence, JIRA).

The Perks: 

Competitive employee rates for your Red Energy electricity & gas bills.
Fixed annual pay increase each year, plus the potential to earn a KPI based bonus on top of your yearly salary.
The option to purchase an additional 4 weeks of annual leave, that’s up to 8 weeks per year.

This is your chance to play a pivotal role in a major business transformation from the ground up. If you're ready to bring your energy, ideas, and initiative into a team that values Ownership, Teamwork, and Agility — we’d love to hear from you. Apply today and help shape the future of energy in Australia.

Are you a strategic yet hands-on data leader ready to own and transform a critical business function?

 

About the Company:

CCM is a fast-growing marketing tech agency that partners with leading technology brands across Asia-Pacific to drive demand, support sales, and deliver high-impact event experiences.

We are a high-energy marketing and telemarketing business that helps ambitious brands build authentic, human-first campaigns that deliver real results. As the business continues through an exciting growth phase, we are investing in the right people, systems and processes to support the next stage of scale. With a collaborative team, supportive leadership and plenty of opportunity to make an impact, CCM offers the chance to be part of a business where data, performance and smart decision-making are central to success.

 

CCM is seeking a Data Operations Lead to manage the full data lifecycle, improve workflows, and ensure campaigns are delivered with speed, precision and compliance.

 

Why Work for Us?

Lead a high-impact function with strategic autonomy

Work with a collaborative, values-driven leadership team

Help shape the future of AI-enabled data workflows

Join a business undergoing exciting growth and transformation

Hybrid work model with flexibility and trust

Rhodes office close to public transport, cafes, and the river

Access to a fully equipped on-site gym

 

The Role:

This newly created role is ideal for someone who thrives in dynamic environments and wants to take ownership of a high-performing data function. As Data Operations Lead, you’ll oversee how data is received, handled, cleaned, structured and delivered across the business, supporting campaign execution, database quality, business reporting, dashboards, and commercial insights.

This role is highly hands-on, with responsibility for day-to-day data operations, workflow optimisation, issue resolution, and practical system improvements that directly impact campaign readiness, reporting quality, and wider business performance. You will also coordinate and oversee offshore support to help ensure data is delivered accurately and on time.

You'll be the go-to expert responsible for ensuring data quality, reliability, while building and continuously improving practical, scalable data processes that support both operational delivery and strategic decision-making. This role suits someone who can manage competing priorities, move quickly, and maintain a high level of accuracy in a deadline-driven campaign environment.

Success in this role will come from delivering accurate, compliant, campaign-ready data on time, improving workflow efficiency, reducing errors, and helping the business build smarter, more scalable processes as it grows.

 

What You’ll Be Doing:

Prepare and deliver compliant, usable datasets based on business and client needs

Own the full data lifecycle – from intake through to structured, usable data

Design and maintain robust data workflows, automations, and integrations

Maintain and improve the quality of CCM’s contact database and underlying data structure

Analyse data trends and performance outcomes to support better business decisions, including around service offering, pricing and operational efficiency

Build, improve and manage dashboards and reporting that support visibility across the business

Lead problem-solving across data systems and processes

Collaborate with key internal stakeholders to optimise database quality, reporting outcomes and business performance

Lead key strategic projects including AI automation, reporting tools, and compliance audits

Continuously improve systems to enhance speed and reduce errors

 

What You’ll Bring:

5+ years in data operations, campaign operations, marketing operations, business analytics or analytics

Proven experience managing high-volume datasets in a fast-paced or deadline-driven environment

Strong experience with contact databases, data quality, enrichment, reporting and performance analysis

Strong technical knowledge of automation tools, data systems, and CRM platforms (preferably HubSpot)

Experience improving workflows and solving operational data issues end-to-end

Strong commercial acumen and problem-solving ability

Excellent stakeholder and communication skills

Experience with data privacy governance preferred

Hands-on mindset – equally comfortable designing strategy and executing it

 

How to Apply:

Apply via the Seek portal. For further information, please contact our external HR Consultant (recruitment@thesavegroup.com.au).

Company description:



We're leading Western Australians to their intelligent energy future.

And we want you to be part of it.

You might know us as Western Australia's largest electricity generator and energy retailer, supporting 1.2 million customers across the state.

But what you might not know, is that we're investing in renewable energy projects, including wind farms and big batteries, to help build a more sustainable and reliable future.

Our big batteries are now live in Kwinana and Collie, we're expanding the Warradarge Wind Farm, building the new King Rocks Wind Farm, and we have many other projects in the works.

We're a purpose-driven organisation, owned by the people of WA. Our commitment is to ensure all Western Australians have access to secure, reliable, and sustainable energy.

We believe that the benefits of renewable energy aren't just for some, they're for everyone.

This is where you come in.



Job description:



Role Description:

The Market Modelling Analyst is a newly created opportunity in our Wholesale Team based at our Central Park, Perth offices.

The Market Modelling Analyst supports Synergy's wholesale strategy by providing analysis, modelling and pricing insights that inform trading, risk and profitability decisions.

The role works across Wholesale market analysis, portfolio modelling, pricing support and performance reporting, contributing to both short-term operational decision-making and longer term strategic planning.

The position contributes to the Wholesale Business Unit's strategic objectives by evaluating the impact of market trends and future scenarios on operational performance, supporting evidence‑based decision‑making, and delivering performance reporting that enables senior leaders to effectively manage risk and value across the wholesale portfolio.

Job responsibilities:

Support wholesale market modelling to evaluate trading and fuel impacts arising from wholesale electricity market trends, portfolio strategies and future scenarios, including analysis of assumptions, sensitivities and outcome drivers.
Support wholesale pricing and risk management activities with a focus on medium‑ to long‑term profitability, including analysis of pricing inputs, portfolio exposures and risk mitigation strategies.
Assist in the development, enhancement and ongoing maintenance of structured modelling frameworks and analytical tools to improve the quality, consistency and transparency of Wholesale Business Unit analysis.
Analyse market, portfolio and operational data to identify trends, emerging risks and opportunities, and provide timely insights to support decision‑making.
Develop, maintain and continuously improve reports and dashboards that provide clear, accurate and meaningful insights into operating and financial performance for senior management.


Profile description:


Tertiary qualification in finance, economics, mathematics, engineering or related analytical discipline
Sound understanding of the Western Australian energy market
Intermediate SQL, Python, R or similar capability, supported by an understanding of data management processes
Experience in financial modelling, pricing and risk analysis
2-4 years' analytical experience in an energy market environment

SAP IBP / APO System Analyst (Supply Chain Planning)
  
12-Month Contract | Enterprise Corporate Environment
Fixed Term contract - 12 months
  
We’re looking for a hands-on SAP planning system analyst to support and enhance an SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) platform within a large, complex FMCG environment.
  
This role will suit someone with a strong SAP APO or SAP planning support background who is either already working with IBP or is looking to transition into SAP IBP with support from an experienced IBP Platform Lead.
  
About the role
You’ll work closely with the IBP Platform Lead and wider business stakeholders to support day-to-day planning operations, system stability, enhancements, testing and upgrades across SAP IBP and its integrated landscape.
  
This is a hands-on system support and enhancement role — ideal for someone who enjoys being close to the system, solving real problems, and working at the intersection of SAP and supply chain planning.
  
What you’ll be doing

Supporting BAU operations across SAP IBP (and related planning tools)
Investigating and resolving incidents, data issues and job failures
Supporting and executing minor configuration changes and enhancements
Working with integration teams across CI-DS / BTP / ECC / BW / APO
Supporting quarterly SAP IBP upgrades and new feature releases
Acting as a bridge between business planners and SAP technical teams
Maintaining documentation, governance and support artefacts
Partnering with the IBP Lead on design decisions and continuous improvement
  
What we’re looking for
We’re open on background, but strong alignment would include:

Experience in SAP IBP or SAP APO (DP, SNP, GATP) or other SAP planning environments
Background in SAP application support, system analysis or functional consulting
Exposure to supply chain planning, demand & supply planning, or S&OP
Experience supporting production SAP systems (incidents, enhancements, upgrades)
Understanding of SAP integrations and data flows
Comfortable working in enterprise, SLA-driven environments
Strong communication skills and ability to work with business stakeholders
  
Nice to have (but not essential):

SAP IBP experience or certification
FMCG or manufacturing environment
Experience transitioning from APO → IBP
  
Why this role

Opportunity to grow into SAP IBP with guidance from an experienced IBP Lead
Large, complex enterprise environment with real planning challenges
Hands-on role with meaningful ownership (not just project admin)
Strong collaboration between IT and business
12-month contract with potential longer-term opportunities
  
Who this role is not for

Pure SAP Finance (FI/CO) profiles
Enterprise architects or program-only leads
Business planners with no SAP system experience

Attractive salary starting from $132,236.00 up to $150,222.00 + 12% Super and annual leave loading

Join the team enriching health in millions of ways every day through digital innovation


Applications Close: 11:59pm, Thursday 23rd April


About Us – Single Digital Patient Record (SDPR), NSW Health 
Be part of the team leading the largest digital transformation in Australia’s public health history. NSW Health’s SDPR program is transforming the way patient care is delivered and experienced - creating a single, secure, holistic, and integrated view of patient information across the entire state.

By connecting every corner of the NSW public health system, this groundbreaking program empowers clinicians and care teams with real-time access to unified patient data - enabling safer, more transparent, and quality care for every person, anywhere.  

Join us now and contribute to the millions of ways we're enriching health every day. 

About the Opportunity 
We're looking for passionate and experienced Senior Digital Health Analysts to join the Single Digital Patient Record Implementation Authority (SDPRIA). We’re currently hiring for multiple positions within our clinical Workstreams of InPatients and Medication, Oncology & Research and building a talent pool for future roles.

The current opportunities available are within the following clinical teams. 
 

Willow
Beacon
Research
Clinical Documentation
Stork (Maternity)
Orders
ASAP 
 

Inpatient Workstream 
The Inpatient Workstream sits at the heart of this reform, reimagining how care is delivered within hospital settings by enabling clinicians to access real-time, integrated patient information at the point of care. This workstream focuses on designing and implementing digital solutions that support the full inpatient journey - from admission to discharge - improving clinical workflows, patient safety, and care coordination.  

By unifying fragmented systems into a single, statewide platform, SDPR empowers clinicians with a holistic view of each patient’s history, treatment, and outcomes, reducing duplication and enhancing decision-making.

Medication, Oncology & Research Workstream
The Medication, Oncology & Research Workstreams are critical pillars of the SDPR program, focused on integrating complex clinical data into a single, secure and accessible patient record. These workstreams enhance visibility of medication management, streamline oncology treatment pathways, and enable richer data capture to support research and innovation.  

By connecting electronic medical records, pathology, and clinical systems, SDPR ensures clinicians have accurate, up-to-date information to make informed decisions and deliver safer, more personalised care.

As a Senior Digital Health Analyst within the Single Digital Patient Record Implementation Authority, you will collaborate closely with clinicians, operational leaders and technical specialists to design and implement digital solutions (including Epic modules) that improve visibility, continuity of care and service efficiency across outpatient and community settings statewide. 

This role offers a unique opportunity to help shape the future of outpatient, ambulatory and community care across NSW by contributing to the SDPR program. You will work on large-scale digital transformation initiatives using platforms such as Epic, partnering with multidisciplinary teams to design, test and optimise solutions that enhance both patient outcomes and clinician experience.

To read more about the opportunities available in the above teams and to view all clinical workstreams within SDPR for potential future roles over the next 12–18 months, please click here.

About You

We are looking for someone who:

Brings tertiary qualifications in a health discipline (Medical, Nursing, Pharmacy, Allied Health, Health Information Management) and/or extensive experience working in a public healthcare environment.
Has strong clinical or public health experience, with a solid understanding of healthcare workflows and models of care.
Can confidently translate frontline clinical requirements into effective digital and technical solutions.
Is detail-oriented, collaborative and comfortable engaging with diverse clinical and non-clinical stakeholders.
Effectively balances competing priorities in complex, fast-paced project environments.
Is adaptable and open to flexible working arrangements, including shift or out-of-hours support when required.

You will succeed in this role if you:  

Enjoy working in an environment where no two days are the same.  
Are motivated by tight deadlines and problem solving on the go.  
Can juggle multiple priorities while staying calm and outcome focused.  
Thrive when collaborating with others to deliver results in a fast moving space.

Our people are at the heart of who we are. We support our people with great benefits so they can enrich health in millions of ways every day:
Professional growth and career development

Salary packaging  
Various health and wellbeing programs to support our team


How to apply

Share with us your cover letter most up to date resume (up to 5 pages), highlighting your relevant skills and experience 

Role-specific enquiries: If you have any questions about the role, please contact the hiring manager Adam Bennet on adam.bennett@health.nsw.gov.au

General recruitment and adjustment enquiries: If you require assistance, have any questions about the recruitment or require adjustments to the recruitment/interview process, please contact Joe Lusaynon on Johar.Lusaynon@health.nsw.gov.au


Additional information 
 

This role may require evening, night, weekend and/or shift work, in accordance with operational needs and Award provisions.
This recruitment may be used to create a Talent Pool for similar future roles (ongoing or temporary) that may arise over the next 18 months.  
To be eligible for this role you must have current Australian work rights (Australian citizen, permanent resident, New Zealand citizen with a current passport, or hold a valid visa with permission to work in Australia).
Successful candidates may be required to complete multiple virtual Epic training sessions and Epic orientation, all scheduled in U.S. time.  
'Exempt' is defined as a fixed term contract with a length greater than 13 weeks.

Ad Text:
A great opportunity to work with one of Australia’s largest general insurers

Be part of a collaborative and innovative data team

Create meaningful impact at an enterprise level by optimizing service delivery

About Hollard   

Hollard Insurance is part of the Hollard Group of Companies and one of Australia’s largest general insurers Australia and New Zealand. We underwrite a broad range of general insurance products, including motor, home, contents, business, bicycle and pet, in proud partnership with some of Australia's biggest consumer brands and broker businesses. As an independent and privately owned company, we believe we can go further together – for our people, customers, communities and partners – through an innovative approach to providing insurance. 

Our state-of-the-art facilities in North Sydney reflect our commitment to providing innovative workspaces that support hybrid and remote work. 

Your Role

You will be responsible for overseeing the delivery of exceptional IT support services to end-users by improving the efficiency, effectiveness, and quality of IT services within an organisation. This involves providing timely and effective resolutions to IT issues and requests, ensuring high levels of user satisfaction. You will be expected to implement robust strategies for optimizing service delivery, ultimately contributing to the organization's overall efficiency and success.

Your duties and responsibilities

Responsible for IT Service delivery within IT Services ensuring IT Services strategy and goals are delivered.

Optimize and drive improvements in IT Service Management practices and processes to deliver the highest level of service, efficiency, resilience, and governance.

Analyse data, user feedback and service delivery trends to gain insights, enable superior IT Services performance and support value-add communication to key stakeholders

Responsible for Identification, Mitigation and Reporting on Problems causing recurring tickets

Ensure smooth transitions of new IT services and updates to existing services, minimising disruption

About You

Demonstrated experience in IT Service Management experience with relevant focus in modern IT operations

Excellent skills on ServiceNow or other leading Ticketing system.

Strong passion for service delivery, process automation, knowledge sharing, collaboration and Agile practices

Good people interaction and stakeholder management skills with the ability to build rapport quickly.

Ability to challenge ideas constructively and suggest possible solutions

Working at Hollard   

At Hollard, you'll discover an environment where you can truly make an impact. We offer the best of both worlds – the opportunity, agility and creativity of a company growing with scale, alongside a focus on becoming a catalyst for enduring, positive, social change.

We're a tight-knit team, where authentic voices and unique perspectives matter. We work supportively, collaboratively and inclusively to help shape our future and create shared value for our communities.

Hollard proudly promotes flexible work including remote work, non-traditional work patterns, and work-family balance. We're also unapologetically committed to creating a workplace that values your diversity with a true sense of inclusion. We actively seek to hire for your different lived experiences, including as it relates to disability, ethnicity, faith, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, gender diversity, sexual orientation, age, neurodiversity.

We're building something special. Working closely with our industry partners to reshape insurance for the better, we're people changing insurance.

Please note: Even if you don't meet all our selection criteria, we encourage you to apply.

A full background check will be completed on the successful applicant.

Australian work rights are required for this position.

Visit the Hollard careers page to learn about how Hollard will collect, store and handle your personal information.

We are NOT engaging Recruitment Agencies for this role and ask agencies to respect our policy of no unsolicited contact regarding advertised vacancies.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
About the Organisation
This ASX-listed organisation (5,000+ employees) is entering an exciting phase of transformation, evolving from a historically decentralised structure into a more connected, enterprise-led model. With multiple business units built through acquisition and growth, they are now investing in a AI Manager to drive AI and automation capability to unlock value at scale across the company, working in a team and supporting the GM. This newly created role sits at the centre of that evolution - shaping how AI drives commercial outcomes across the business.

The Role

Reporting into the General Manager, Group Data & AI, this is a leadership role responsible for translating AI strategy into measurable business outcomes. Operating within a hub-and-spoke model, you will work alongside the GM to lead the development of a "Group" AI capability, partnering closely with business units while driving 2-3 company-wide initiatives and reporting on ROI to executives.

Key responsibilities include:

Work with the GM to drive and create the Group AI 3-year roadmap, prioritising initiatives aligned to strategic transformation goals
Identify high-value opportunities and develop robust business cases with clear ROI
Engage senior stakeholders to understand business challenges and translate them into AI-driven solutions
Drive transformation initiatives, embedding AI into operations - work alongside technical PM's and leads in the business units
Establish and track KPIs and value realisation metrics across programs
Provide executive-level reporting on progress, risks and commercial outcomes
Lead cross-functional discovery workshops and coordinate delivery across internal teams and external partners
Act as the AI evangelist, driving adoption, capability uplift and organisational change
Build and grow a small, high-performing team (initially 0-2 reports, and scaling over time)


This role is highly strategic (~80%) with a strong focus on stakeholder engagement, transformation and value delivery. You'll be supported by technologists in the business.

About You

Proven experience leading transformation projects in Data, AI, Digital or Product environments
Strong commercial acumen, with a track record of delivering measurable business outcomes
Exceptional stakeholder engagement skills, with experience influencing at Executive level (GM and CxO)
Demonstrated ability to build business cases and articulate ROI for complex initiatives
Solid understanding of AI, Machine Learning and Data capabilities and how to apply them in real-world settings - curiosity and passion to carve your career in AI
A natural change agent - curious, forward-thinking and commercially pragmatic
Experience working in complex, multi-business unit environments
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts into business value


Nice to Have

Industry experience in utilities, telco, or engineering
Exposure to AWS or cloud-based AI solutions
Product or project management certifications (Agile, PMP, PRINCE2 etc.)


Why Apply?

Newly created role with genuine influence and a driver of change
Opportunity to shape a 3-year AI roadmap from the ground up
Work directly with senior leadership and executive stakeholders
Strong investment in AI, with existing use cases and momentum across the business
Competitive package including:
Share scheme
Discretionary bonus
Training & development
Leave buy-back
Product discounts


For a confidential discussion, please contact Kylie.McManus@talentintenational.com / 0408 388 680 or apply via the link.

Category: Corporate Finance & Investment Banking (Banking & Financial Services)
Ad Text:
Company description:



Zurich is a leading multi-line insurer that serves its customers in global and local markets. With about 55,000 employees, it provides a wide range of property and casualty, life insurance products and services in more than 215 countries and territories. Zurich's customers include individuals, small businesses, and mid-sized and large companies, as well as multinational corporations.



Job description:



A new transformational adventure awaits you…

How does 5 weeks leave per year, an impactful career that you'll truly love, and working for a trustworthy organisation that empowers wellbeing and is accelerating digital transformation sound like to you?

As one of Forbes 2024 ‘World's Best Employers' and Fortune's ‘World's Most Admired Companies' for 2025, Zurich - a leader in the insurance industry - is on a journey towards a brighter future… A brighter future for you - focused on your career, your wellbeing, and your community - as well as our customers and the planet.

This exciting adventure will drive positive impact towards our vision to be Australia's leading digital insurer. So, if you want to be part of a values led global organisation, where real growth and transformation is happening, along with our track record of making a real difference of continuously taking steps toward a sustainable future for our people, customers, communities, and our planet…read on!

Let's create a brighter future together, let's make a difference

It's important to know, what you do matters. At Zurich, we don't just cover, we care.

A great opportunity exists to join Zurich's Investment Management team as an Investment Analyst and play an integral role in managing approximately A$8 billion in assets across our Australian Life and General Insurance balance sheet assets. Reporting to the Chief Investment Officer (ZFSA), this position sits at the heart of our balance sheet investment function.

You'll support the execution of strategic asset allocation and investment management across fixed income, equities, private debt, and property, while contributing to investment strategy, risk management, and sustainable income outcomes. Working closely with senior stakeholders, you'll assist with external manager selection and oversight, performance monitoring, and mandate compliance.

This is a broad, analytical role offering exposure to portfolio analysis, capital deployment, forecasting, Net Investment Income (NII) reporting, and the preparation of Investment Committee and regulatory materials. It's an ideal opportunity for someone looking to deepen their investment capability within a global organisation driving meaningful transformation.

Important to your success - Unlock your potential

Tertiary qualifications Economics, Commerce, Business Administration, Actuarial Studies, or another relevant discipline.
Progress toward a CFA designation is desirable (or CA/CPA/FIAA). Candidates should be willing to enrol in the CFA program if not already commenced.
Minimum 4 years' experience in a relevant role within banking, insurance, or asset management (financial services industry).
Strong experience supporting investment decision‑making through analysis and forecasting.
Proven capability in preparing or contributing to Investment Committee papers and regulatory reporting
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills and the ability to clearly articulate complex details.
Strong technical capabilities, including financial modelling, analysis, and reporting.

Belong. Zurich is here to support you

There are so many reasons why Zurich is a great place to be and the right choice for you, but here are just a few.

5 weeks leave per year for permanent staff through our Z leave initiative, as well as 15 days personal/carer's leave (as we empower our people to manage their own wellbeing), plus access to Family Care (parental leave)
Hybrid working arrangement - office 3 days and WFH 2 days p/w standard for FT employees
Access programs to stay healthy and feel healthier along with receiving a AUD$500 wellbeing payment per year, plus an AUD$295 new starter e-voucher for your WFH set up (flex work - hybrid working), and other great benefits through our Shine portal
Grow, and thrive in your career (we support and encourage it) with award winning training programs. As our strength is our people and we value your individuality
Work with global, diverse, and inclusive teams; Explore your passions, fuel innovation, drive equity, collaborate and make a positive difference beyond your role
Global mobility opportunities across more than 200 countries worldwide
Volunteer and work in our state-of-the-art Green Star rated offices (when you are in the office) and dress for your day
We plant a tree for every new employee
Not to mention our various employer of choice awards/memberships - WGEA; Family Inclusive workplace; Gold AWEI employer, Pride in diversity… to name a few.

We could go on, but the main point is that Zurich is a great place to be, where you can truly belong, be yourself, have work/life balance, and thrive in a supportive environment.

Zurich is an equal opportunity employer.  We are committed to ensuring that our recruitment process is fair and accessible for all candidates. If you require any special accommodations to participate in our recruitment process, we encourage you to please let us know at the time of your application.

So, if you want to come on an adventure that will build a brighter future together, and feel a sense of togetherness, then please apply today to start your adventure!

Job Title: FP&A Manager | Global SaaS | Private Equity
Ad Text:
About the Company

This is a rare opportunity to join a high-growth, private equity-backed global SaaS business that is scaling rapidly across multiple international markets.

This business delivers a sophisticated cloud-based platform to enterprise and mid-market clients worldwide. With strong financial backing and a clear growth mandate, the organisation is entering its next phase of expansion, focused on scaling revenue, enhancing product capability and driving operational excellence.

The culture is a collaborative, high-performing and flexible, with a strong emphasis on trust, autonomy and outcomes. As a remote-first organisation, they offer significant flexibility while maintaining a highly connected and engaged global team.

The Opportunity

Reporting directly to the CFO, this is a high-impact FP&A leadership role at the centre of the business.

You'll play a critical role in shaping financial strategy, driving performance and influencing key commercial decisions across the organisation. This is a true business partnering position with exposure to senior leadership, investors and global stakeholders.

You'll also have the opportunity to contribute to ad hoc M&A activity, long-term strategic planning and the ongoing evolution of the company's data and analytics capability.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the group FP&A function, delivering high-quality monthly reporting, board packs and SaaS performance insights
Own and develop SaaS metrics and KPIs to drive deeper understanding of business performance
Partner closely with leaders across Sales, Marketing, Product, Technology and Operations to support decision-making and performance improvement
Deliver robust budgeting, forecasting and 3-way financial models, aligned to strategic objectives
Provide commercial insight and analysis to identify opportunities, optimise costs and improve profitability
Drive improvements in reporting, systems and financial processes across the business
Champion data-driven decision making, including ownership of reporting tools (e.g. Power BI) and collaboration with data teams
Support the CFO on M&A activity, business cases and strategic initiatives
Lead and mentor junior team members, building capability across FP&A and analytics

About You

You're a commercially minded FP&A professional who thrives in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

Proven experience in FP&A / commercial finance, ideally within SaaS, tech or a high-growth business
Strong understanding of SaaS metrics and revenue models
Advanced financial modelling and analytical capability
Ability to influence senior stakeholders and translate data into clear commercial insights
Experience working in private equity-backed or investor-led environments (highly regarded)
Exposure to BI tools (Power BI, Snowflake, etc.) and data-driven environments
A proactive, curious mindset with a genuine interest in driving business performance

Why Join?

True strategic role with direct exposure to CFO, board and investors
Global business with strong growth trajectory and PE backing
Remote-first environment with genuine flexibility and work-life balance
High-performance culture that values collaboration, autonomy and innovation
Opportunity to be part of major growth initiatives, including M&A and scaling operations

If you're looking for a role where you can shape the direction of a business, this is one of the more exciting FP&A opportunities currently in the Melbourne market.

Group Ventures Development Specialist

 Kensington, Sydney | 2-Year Fixed Term | Full-Time


Shape the future of education, build what doesn't exist yet.

At UNSW College, we don’t just deliver education- we open pathways, create opportunity, and expand access to world-class learning.

As part of this mission, UNSW Group Ventures is a newly established function focused on identifying and delivering major international partnerships and venture opportunities across the UNSW ecosystem, including all university faculties, institutes, entrepreneurship function, lifelong learning and College. 

 We’re now looking for a Group Ventures Development Specialist to help bring these opportunities to life.




Why this role is different

This isn’t a traditional university role.

You’ll be working within a startup-style function inside a globally recognised institution, where ideas move quickly, priorities shift, and no two days look the same.

It is particularly exciting as the role involves working across a wide range of international markets, offering global exposure and the opportunity to shape meaningful partnerships in different contexts. 

If you’re coming from consulting or a fast-paced commercial environment and want something equally engaging - but more meaningful and sustainable - this is your opportunity.




What you’ll be doing

You’ll play a key role across the full venture lifecycle -supporting initiatives from early-stage concept through to execution.

This includes:

• Analysing opportunities 

o Conduct market research and synthesise complex data into clear insights 

o Evaluate global education trends, partnerships, and commercial models 

• Shaping strategic outputs 

o Develop business cases, analytical briefs, and executive-ready papers 

o Translate complexity into structured, decision-ready materials 

• Supporting governance & delivery 

o Prepare Board and committee documentation 

o Coordinate inputs across finance, legal, and academic stakeholders 

• Driving operational progress 

o Support due diligence, reporting, and pipeline tracking 

o Contribute to improving processes, frameworks, and ways of working 

• Engaging stakeholders 

o Support internal and external collaboration across a highly matrixed environment 




What we’re looking for

We’re not just hiring for skills -we’re hiring for mindset and capability.

You’ll likely bring:

• Experience in consulting, commercial operations, or complex project environments 

• Strong analytical and written communication skills (you can turn complexity into clarity) 

• Experience supporting multi-stakeholder initiatives 

• The ability to manage multiple priorities and adapt quickly 

• High attention to detail and strong organisational discipline 




And just as importantly…

You are:

• Adaptable – you can pivot quickly across markets, priorities, and problems 

• Intellectually curious – you enjoy solving complex, ambiguous challenges 

• Proactive – you anticipate what’s needed before being asked 

• Collaborative – you work across boundaries and build strong relationships 

• Grounded but ambitious – you want to build something meaningful 

Bonus points if you have experience in:

• Consulting environments 

• International education or global market development 

• Governance, risk, or regulatory frameworks 

• Tenders, partnerships, or commercial negotiations 




What you’ll get in return

• The opportunity to build and shape a new function from the ground up 

• Exposure to high-impact, global initiatives 

• Work that is strategic, analytical, and genuinely meaningful 

• A collaborative, high-performing team that values curiosity and initiative 

• The stability and reputation of UNSW, with the energy of a startup 




To understand the scope of the benefits we offer please, visit our Careers Page - https://www.unswcollege.edu.au/careers




Ready to apply?

Follow the prompts below: 

• A CV detailing your qualifications and experience. 

• A Cover letter briefly addressing the selection criteria. 




Applications close at midnight on the Thursday, April 30.  




Questions? jobs@unswcollege.edu.au 




If you’re looking for a role where you can think, build, analyse, and influence at a global level, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now and be part of something that’s just getting started.

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues.​ We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members. We are dedicated to building a high-performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.

Your New Role

As a Member Portfolio Business Analyst, you'll play a key role in helping AustralianSuper deliver meaningful change for members by supporting portfolio planning, prioritisation and delivery across the Member Domain.

Reporting to the Senior Manager - Portfolio Planning, you'll work closely with sponsors, initiative owners, subject matter experts and enabling teams to assess, shape and progress initiatives through the Member Portfolio. You'll bring strong business analysis capability to support discovery, define requirements, assess impacts and ensure initiatives are set up for success.

This role sits at the intersection of strategy, delivery and continuous improvement, with a strong focus on collaboration, value‑based decision making and simplifying how we operate to deliver better member outcomes.

Key responsibilities include:

Supporting Member Portfolio Planning activities, including backlog management, triage and prioritisation
Managing the domain backlog, engagement hub and front‑door processes
Supporting initiative assessment, discovery and prioritisation using data‑led frameworks
Partnering with initiative owners to develop clear problem definitions, business cases and benefits
Conducting high‑level business analysis, including documenting business, process and information requirements
Supporting portfolio reporting, impact assessments and change governance
Contributing to efficiency, simplification and standardised ways of working across the Member Domain
Building trusted relationships with stakeholders across the Fund to enable successful delivery

What You'll Need

5+ years working in a general Business Analysis
Strong experience in superannuation operations essential
Extensive experience in business analysis, ideally within superannuation or financial services
Strong understanding of portfolio, initiative and change management practices
Demonstrated experience eliciting, analysing and documenting business and process requirements
Proven ability to engage, influence and manage senior and cross‑functional stakeholders
Experience supporting discovery initiatives, business cases and benefits realisation
Strong analytical, problem‑solving and communication skills
Ability to work across multiple initiatives, prioritise effectively and operate with a risk‑aware mindset
Relevant tertiary qualification and/or formal training in Business Analysis

Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next

Apply now, if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes and would like the opportunity to work in a challenging, growing and rapidly evolving team to deliver outstanding results.

Job Title: Head of Research, Innovation and Predictive Capability
Ad Text:
CFA is an emergency service that places the community at the centre of everything we do. We work to protect lives and property across Victoria, every hour of the day.

 

Our organisation is made up of employees working alongside more than 51,000 volunteers. We value the different skills, experience and viewpoints that people bring, because they strengthen how we operate and support the services Victorians rely on.

We aim to be a workplace where people feel supported to do meaningful work, develop professionally and contribute to outcomes that matter to the people and places we serve.

To learn more about who we are and the work we do, visit our About Us page.

About the Role

The Head of Research, Innovation and Predictive Capability is a newly created executive leadership role reporting to the Deputy Chief Officer, Fire Risk Research and Community Preparedness. This position provides strategic leadership across CFA, government and the broader sector to strengthen bushfire management practices in Victoria and reduce the impacts of bushfires on people, the environment and the economy.

The role leads key functions including the CFA research program, sector innovation initiatives, the joint-agency Predictive Services Program and bushfire risk analysis. This includes guiding research priorities, driving innovation and the adoption of new technologies, strengthening predictive capability and supporting evidence-based decision making through data, field risk assessments and risk modelling.

As a member of the senior leadership team, you will influence research and innovation across the sector, build strong partnerships with government, agencies and research organisations and provide strategic advice to support CFA's bushfire risk management responsibilities. The role also contributes to the broader strategic direction of the organisation while fostering collaboration and leading with professionalism, integrity and accountability.

About you

You are a highly experienced and strategic manager with strong expertise in natural resource management, fire-related research or a related field. A tertiary qualification in a relevant discipline is essential, with a PhD or management qualification highly regarded.

You bring extensive experience leading the development and delivery of complex programs and initiatives, with a proven ability to translate strategy into outcomes that support organisational goals. Established networks in research, innovation and predictive services are essential, with connections in the fire and emergency services sector highly desirable.

You are a skilled leader, capable of building and guiding high-performing teams and have a strong track record in developing effective partnerships, networks and community-centred initiatives. Your exceptional communication and interpersonal skills enable you to navigate complex matters and influence internal and external stakeholders with credibility and impact.

Why choose CFA

Meaningful Purpose: Your contribution truly makes a difference
Flexibility: Hybrid work options with flexible work arrangements
Discounts: Emergency Memberlink discounts on various services
Wellbeing Focus: Health Ready programs, flu vaccinations
Member Assistance Program: Access support across 8 service pathways 

Your Application

Click the ‘Apply' button to access our online application portal. You will be prompted to provide your personal and contact details and to respond to employment-related questions. When applying for a position with CFA, please include a cover letter and resume that clearly demonstrate your qualifications, experience and the key attributes and skills relevant to the role.

Pre-employment Checks

Offers of employment will only be made to candidates who are able to successfully complete a Nationally Coordinated Criminal History Check, Working Rights Check and hold a valid Working with Children Check.  You will also be required to provide at least two professional references.

Applications close: 11:59pm 3 May 2026

CFA is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse, inclusive, and safe volunteer and work environment. Our aim is to have a volunteer and paid workforce that reflects the community it serves.  First Nations people, women, people of all ages, with disabilities and culturally and linguistically diverse people are encouraged to apply.

The Country Fire Authority (CFA) values your privacy. We will use the information you provide when you are applying for employment with us for the purpose of recruitment, selection, evaluation and appointment and to report on anonymised key metrics (e.g. diversity). The information we collect will be handled in accordance with privacy laws, including the Privacy and Data Protection Act 2014. You can view our Privacy Statement here. Who We Are

 

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

 

What You’ll Be Doing 

 

As an Engineering Delivery Lead, you will drive the successful delivery of technical initiatives and continuous improvement across software engineering squads. You will act as the operational link between business and technical teams, ensuring effective execution of project pipelines, release management, and delivery governance.

 

Supporting multiple delivery pods, you will translate strategic intent into clear, actionable work, remove blockers, facilitate agile rituals, and improve flow efficiency to enable high-quality delivery aligned to commercial and customer outcomes.

 

Delivery Execution & Coordination: Lead end-to-end software delivery for cross-functional teams, ensuring timely, high-quality outcomes across the SDLC. Facilitate agile ceremonies, promote delivery best practices, manage backlogs, and translate high-level initiatives into sprint-ready work with clear outcomes.
Stakeholder Management & Collaboration: Partner with Engineering Managers, Product Owners, and Project Managers to set priorities and manage delivery plans. Provide clear stakeholder communication through structured updates and reporting (e.g. RAID), ensuring effective collaboration and handovers between business and technical teams.
Governance & Compliance: Ensure delivery aligns with established governance and CTM Flow, meeting all commercial, security, and regulatory requirements through structured, auditable processes.
Continuous Improvement & Metrics: Monitor delivery health through velocity, risk, and dependency tracking. Proactively address blockers and lead improvements in planning accuracy, estimation, quality, and delivery efficiency.
Team Enablement & Culture: Support Engineering Managers in building team capability and a high-performance culture, fostering continuous learning, agile ways of working, and knowledge sharing across teams.

 

                                                                                                                                          

What’s In It For You

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours.  We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA, and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

 

Vibrant and social community who have annual celebrations, family fun days and regular events
Enjoy flexible work arrangements, including the option of one day working from home each week or a 9-day fortnight
Enjoy additional leave days - ‘ME’ leave and ‘Volunteer Day’ leave
Option to purchase additional leave
Return To Work bonus for returning parents
Quarterly Award and Recognition program

 

 

What Sets You Apart

 

Technology related Degree or relevant industry experience. Formal certification is desirable.

Experience:

3+ years in software delivery roles (e.g., Delivery Lead, Scrum Master or Iteration Manager)
Hands-on experience facilitating agile delivery, managing release cycles, and simultaneously supporting multiple cross-functional technical teams
Understanding of SDLC processes and Agile frameworks (Scrum, Kanban)

Job Title: Senior Talent Growth and Leadership Partner
Ad Text:
What's the role?

In this position, you will partner closely with HR and business stakeholders to design and deliver high impact, scalable learning interventions that directly support our strategic priorities. Your responsibilities include:

Strategic Capability Planning: Designing and implementing a cohesive annual learning strategy that clarifies desired skills and proficiency levels across all employee cohorts.

Curriculum Design & Delivery(facilitation): Leading the development of innovative learning programs that translate theory into practical, on-the-job skills.

Leadership Development: Engaging with leaders to deliver specific development programs for emerging leaders and specialised workshops.

Supplier & Content Management: Selecting and managing external learning suppliers and maintaining a centralised repository learning resources.

Project Oversight: Planning and prioritising multiple development activities to meet objectives within specified timescales and budgets.

Who are you?

We are looking for a strategic thinker who can translate complex business objectives into effective learning solutions. You should possess:

Consultative Business Partnering: A proactive approach with the ability to challenge the status quo and drive constructive change.

Stakeholder Influence: Exceptional relationship-building skills and the ability to consult effectively with varied levels of leadership.

Design Expertise: Strong knowledge of adult learning principles, blended learning methodologies, and contemporary instructional design models.

Strategic Mindset: The ability to see ahead to future possibilities and prioritise initiatives for the greatest organisational impact.

Effective Communication: Excellent verbal and written skills, with the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for any audience.

Commitment to Excellence: A results-driven attitude that consistently achieves goals and holds self and others accountable to high standards.

Innovative Spirit: An openness to new ideas and a drive to apply innovative methods, tools, and technology to learning.

Why News Corp Australia

Join a culture where innovation is our operating rhythm. You’ll work on a diverse portfolio of Australia’s most loved brands and play a pivotal role in leading the technological shift in how we create commercial content. Our vibrant, hybrid work environment and dynamic, values-driven culture empowers you to push boundaries, collaborate as one unified team, and make a positive, measurable impact for all Australians.

News Extras Benefits is a commitment to our people for their valued contribution to News, our audiences, clients and communities.

Our benefits include:

Exclusively ours - Access to publications and products from across the News Corp family

Perks & discounts - Exclusive offers across a range of products and services that deliver genuine cost savings and benefits

Health & wellbeing - Advice, support and facilities for healthy minds and bodies (including an onsite gym at our Adelaide, Brisbane & Sydney offices)

Time off - News offers a range of leave options to support your personal needs and moments that matter in life: Parental leave, Birthday leave, Volunteer leave, Career break and Compassionate leave.

Financial wellbeing - Support on making the most of your money

In addition to our perks and benefits, employees have access to a number of initiatives and programs that support their wellbeing and professional growth, such as:

Helping you grow - Access tailored development opportunities, including leadership programs, to cultivate your skills and support your career

Giving back - Make a difference to our communities through volunteer opportunities and company-organised charity drives

Workplace Inclusion and facilities – Engage with your passions through various employee-resource groups and accessibility to multi-faith quiet rooms & parents’ rooms

Who are we?

This role is part of the Human Resources (HR) team where our people vision is to unlock News’ potential for growth in an ever-changing media landscape, we equip passionate people with the skills to excel, supported by an innovative and collaborative environment. Our people’s ideas and unique perspectives take centre stage, celebrated and delivered through the widest range of media brands and channels in Australia.

We’re focused on delivering the News Corp Australia strategy through delivering a future ready workforce and an innovative and dynamic workplace with automated, seamless and personalised experiences in a sustainable, high performing organisation

What’s next?

Apply now or for more information on careers at News Corp Australia visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Diversity strengthens our national teams and newsroom as we seek to tell stories that represent the communities of Australia. Our people make us unique, and so we are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, religion, sexual orientation, age, disability or gender identity. Applicants of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply!

We are committed to ensuring an accessible and supportive recruitment process. If you require any adjustments or accommodations at any stage, please do not hesitate to let us know here. In order for us to assist you, please ensure you include the role title you are applying for in the subject line. For more information regarding Diversity at NewsCorp Australia please visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers under the Diversity@News section.

Job Title: Client Partner, Delivery Lead - Data & Technology
Ad Text:
We’ve been retained to appoint a Senior Client Partner to join a growing Sydney-based team, leading the delivery of complex programs while supporting key client relationships and growth.

This is a delivery-led role at its core. You will be responsible for overseeing and guiding programs of work end-to-end, ensuring quality outcomes, strong governance, and successful execution. Alongside delivery, you will contribute to account development and identify opportunities for expansion within existing clients.

The Role
You will play a pivotal role in leading delivery across multiple engagements, acting as the senior point of accountability for outcomes. While you will be aligned to key clients, the focus is on delivering high-quality work and building long-term trust through execution.

This is not a pure sales or account management role. Your credibility will come from your ability to lead delivery and consistently achieve results.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the delivery of complex programs across strategy and technology implementation
Take accountability for outcomes across the full delivery lifecycle
Provide oversight, structure, and governance across multiple engagements
Build strong, trusted relationships through consistent delivery excellence
Support account strategy and identify opportunities for growth within existing clients
Collaborate with sales and pursuit teams to shape opportunities where relevant

About You

You are a senior leader/manager with a strong track record of delivering programs, not just managing relationships.

Proven experience leading large-scale or complex program delivery
Background in consulting, ideally at Senior Manager or Principal level
Strong understanding of delivery frameworks, governance, and execution
Ability to engage stakeholders across both business and technology
Commercial awareness, with the ability to spot opportunities through delivery
Motivated by ownership, accountability, and delivering outcomes

Why This Role

This is an opportunity to step into a senior role where delivery is the foundation of your impact.

You’ll lead meaningful programs of work while contributing to the growth of key client relationships, without being purely sales-driven.

About the role
Chariot Corporation Ltd (ASX: CC9) is a lithium exploration company with a growing portfolio of assets across Nigeria and the United States. As we scale rapidly toward our ambition of becoming a leading lithium supplier, we're looking for a Corporate Development Analyst to join our Perth-based team.

You'll work side-by-side with senior executives, directly influencing M&A activity, capital markets strategy, and high-value partnerships — all while building a career with tangible, visible impact in one of the world's most critical supply chains

This is an opportunity to work side-by-side with senior executives, directly influence M&A, capital markets and strategic partnerships—and build a career with real impact.

What you'll be doing

Build financial models and valuation analyses for investments, JV structures, and transactions

Support due diligence across legal, financial, and operational dimensions

Contribute to deal execution, including contract negotiation and project management

Draft ASX announcements, investor materials, and disclosure documents

Support the legal and company secretarial teams on compliance and governance

Stay sharp on market trends, regulatory shifts, and risks shaping the global lithium industry




What we're looking for

Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Law, Engineering (other mining related technical field) with strong academic results

1–5 years' experience in corporate development, investment banking, strategy consulting, or resources

Strong financial modelling and Excel skills—comfortable running valuations and scenario analysis

Strong communication skills—clear, concise, and confident in stakeholder settings

Experience in mining, energy, or professional services is highly regarded

Australian citizenship or permanent residency required

What we offer


Competitive base salary + performance bonuses and equity incentives

Exposure to transformational M&A and growth projects

Hands-on experience across mining, capital markets, and strategy

Opportunities for international travel (USA, West Africa)

A lean, ambitious team where your impact is visible and valued


About us
Chariot Resources Limited is a mineral exploration company focused on discovering and developing high-grade and near surface lithium opportunities focused principally in the United States and Nigeria.




In addition to the recently announced acquisition of a Nigerian lithium portfolio, Chariot has twelve (12) lithium projects in the United States.


Chariot’s USA projects include the Black Mountain Project (which is prospective for hard rock lithium) in Wyoming, USA and the Resurgent Project (which is prospective for claystone lithium) in Nevada and Oregon, USA.


The Nigerian portfolio of hard-rock lithium assets consists of four project clusters (Fonlo, Gbugbu, Iganna, and Saki) in the Oyo and Kwara states which cover approximately 254 square kilometers and are comprised of 8 exploration licences and 2 small-scale mining leases. These assets represent one of the largest portfolios of lithium assets in the country and have a history of significant artisanal lithium mining.

Chariot holds a portfolio interest in certain properties prospective for claystone hosted lithium located in the State of Nevada in the United States through its interest in Mustang Lithium LLC.
